data.title: 'Don't Be a Hater,' Says Top Investor About Super Micro Computer Stock
data.description:
The verdict was swift and painful last October for Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) when the company shared that its Q1 2026 revenues had hit a bump in the road. In a note released just a few days prior to its earnings call, SMCI reported that "design wins" had pushed some expected sales for Q1 into the following quarter.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While some companies might receive the benefit of the doubt when sharing a hiccup or two, clearly SMCI isn't one of them. Though things have improved a bit in 2026, SMCI's share price has lost some 40% of its value over the past three months.

In a sense, it seems that SMCI has yet to fully recover from the negative vibes around its previous accounting and compliance issues. However, one top investor known by the pseudonym Stone Fox Capital doesn't think that SMCI deserves to be "the most hated AI stock."

"Investors should use the weakness to load up on the stock with the ability to both beat FY27 targets and rerate based on the boosted numbers," states the 5-star investor, who is among the top 3% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Stone Fox points out that the demand for data centers remains piping hot. The investor cites a recent announcement from Meta Platforms' CEO Mark Zuckerberg, who shared his company's intention to build hundreds of GW or more as part of its "Meta Compute" initiative.

Moreover, McKinsey & Company has forecasted that data center capacity will surge from 82 GW in 2025 to 219 GW in 2030, notes Stone Fox. The investor also mentions that much of this spend will be on technologies that SMCI is working on.

The huge capex spend meshes with SMCI's forecast for future orders, further details Stone Fox. Therefore, Stone Fox expects the company to beat what the investor deems "relatively flat" revenue projections in the coming two years.

"My investment thesis remains ultra-bullish on the stock, especially after falling back to yearly lows despite ongoing strong AI server demand," sums up Stone Fox, who rates SMCI a Buy. (To watch Stone Fox Capital's track record, click here)

Wall Street, as Stone Fox mentions, isn't exactly showering SMCI with love. With 5 Buys, 5 Holds, and 2 Sells, SMCI carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Its 12-month average price target of $44.82 would translate into gains of ~37%. (See SMCI stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: 'It's Not Too Late,' Says Top Investor About Intel Stock
data.description:
There are plenty of superlatives that could describe the path of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) during 2025. The company flipped the script on a disappointing period, delivering an impressive set of achievements that included a new partnership with Nvidia and a strategic alignment (and investment) from the U.S. government.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier this month, Intel launched its Core Ultra Series 3 processors, which the company trumpeted as "the most advanced semiconductor process ever developed and manufactured in the United States."

The company's share price has skyrocketed over the past year, more than doubling during the past twelve months. The burner has continued this year as well, and INTC is up by almost 30% in the first few weeks of 2026.

On the heels of such a striking reversal of fortune – and just before the company shares its Q4 2025 numbers on January 22 – is it too late to jump on board? Not according to top investor Timothy Green, who believes INTC still has room to run.

"While shares of Intel have surged since the start of 2025, the company's market capitalization is still well below its all-time high, and even below multi-year highs reached in early 2020," explains the 5-star investor, who is among the top 3% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Green highlights three main drivers of future growth: the launch of Panther Lake, the demand for its server CPUs, and the company's long-term foundry opportunity.

Regarding Panther Lake, the investor cites the improved performance and efficiency of the new chip, along with its enhanced battery life and "huge leap in graphics capabilities." Green notes that Nova Lake is slated to follow Panther Lake, and he also mentions the potential Serpent Lake PC CPU that is rumored to be the codename for Intel's joint efforts with Nvidia.

Another factor behind the investor's optimism is due to improvements in the company's CPU offerings. Green points out that Intel has nearly sold out its server CPUs for 2026.

"With strong demand and major performance and efficiency improvements likely this year and next as Intel launches new chips, the company should see solid revenue and profit growth in its data center segment," adds Green.

Lastly, the investor cites the opportunity for Intel's foundry to generate meaningful business from external customers, though he also acknowledges that this will take some time.

Therefore, though he warns that it won't always be smooth sailing, Green is along for the long-term ride.

"Intel stock has shot up quickly, and volatility will likely be par for the course. But in the long run, Intel looks like a great investment as the company stages a comeback," concludes Green. (To watch Timothy Green's track record, click here)

That's far from the consensus view on Wall Street, however, which seems to think that the upward momentum will be slowing down. With 8 Buys, 19 Holds, and 4 Sells, INTC carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Its 12-month average price target of $43.37 implies a potential downside of ~8% in the year ahead. (See INTC stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Trump Says No Need to 'Think Purely of Peace' After Noble Peace Prize Snub
data.description:
In a letter to Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre, President Donald Trump signaled that his failure to win the Nobel Peace Prize increased his interest in acquiring Greenland.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Considering your Country decided not to give me the Nobel Peace Prize for having stopped 8 Wars PLUS, I no longer feel an obligation to think purely of Peace, although it will always be predominant," Trump wrote.

Trump Slaps New Tariffs on Europe over Greenland Push

Last week, Trump announced an additional 10% tariff on eight European countries, including Norway, Denmark, and the United Kingdom. The tariff will remain in effect until the U.S. reaches a deal to purchase Greenland and will be enforced starting on February 1 with an increase to 25% on June 1.

The European Union (EU) has vowed to retaliate and is considering imposing $103 billion in tariffs on U.S. goods. The bloc is also weighing activating its anti-coercion tool, which could result in tariffs and new taxes on U.S. companies doing business in Europe.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'No Longer Just an Auto Company,' Cathie Wood Says of Tesla (TSLA)
data.description:
Ark Invest CEO and longtime Tesla (TSLA) bull Cathie Wood asserted that the company has evolved beyond cars, pivoting to robotaxis. In a recent CNBC Squawk Box interview, she noted that despite pressure on global electric vehicle (EV) sales, investors are shifting focus to Tesla's emerging robotaxi business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wood described Tesla's current phase as a transition from low-margin, traditional automotive business to a high-margin software-as-a-service (SaaS) model, driven by autonomous ride-hailing. She highlighted that Tesla is moving from achieving modest gross margins of approximately 15% on automotive hardware to a higher-margin business that promises 70% to 80% margins, fueled by recurring revenue from autonomous ride-hailing services.

Analysts Are Incorporating Robotaxi Now

Wood contends that analysts are progressively integrating these robotaxi dynamics into their valuation frameworks now, making Tesla's true potential clearer. She noted that recent upgrades, even from auto-focused analysts consulting tech peers, signal growing consensus that Tesla is a tech innovator, not just a carmaker.

CNBC also challenged Wood on the specifics of Tesla's autonomous robotaxis and humanoid robots, questioning whether these ambitious initiatives might require years to achieve meaningful scale. Wood countered by pointing to Tesla's rapid expansion in operational footprint, which she believes outpaces general expectations.

She highlighted the competitive dynamic in Texas, where Tesla and Alphabet's (GOOGL) Waymo are directly vying for territory, a development she views positively as it accelerates innovation. She forecasts faster robotaxi rollout than analysts expect, especially if federal rules replace state fragmentation.

Is Tesla a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Analysts remain sidelined on Tesla stock currently. On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $397.47 implies 9.2% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained 3.2%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: The Stock Market Is Closed on MLK Day, but Here's What Goldman Sachs Says to Buy When Trading Resumes
data.description:
The stock market isn't open on Martin Luther King Jr. Day, but that doesn't mean investors are sitting idle. While markets are closed in observance of the holiday, Wall Street's focus is already shifting toward what could be top trades once trading resumes.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

One name drawing attention from Goldman Sachs analyst Asad Haider is BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX). On Friday, Haider upgraded the stock from Neutral to Buy and lifted his 12-month price target to $142 from $115, implying 30% upside from current levels.

Haider believes BioNTech sits "at the nexus of significant developments occurring in the oncology field," pointing to the company's expanding footprint across next-generation immuno-oncology and antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). In Haider's view, that positioning could give BioNTech a meaningful role in what the analyst describes as the "next wave" of cancer treatment innovation – well beyond the company's legacy COVID-19 vaccine business.

Importantly, the bullish call isn't built around a single asset or trial. Haider highlights BioNTech's broad, strategic portfolio, which includes novel PD-1/VEGF bispecifics, ADC programs, and mRNA-based cancer vaccines. The analyst argues this diversified approach allows the company to pursue multiple shots on goal across large oncology indications, while also reducing dependence on any single readout.

While COVID-related revenue continues to generate meaningful cash flow, Haider believes the oncology pipeline is set to become the more material driver of stock performance. The analyst notes that BioNTech shares have lagged in 2025, in part due to lingering overhang from the COVID franchise. However, he sees the narrative beginning to shift as the company moves deeper into late-stage development, with a series of clinical milestones expected to build into 2026.

In fact, Haider points to 2026 as the start of a multi-year catalyst-rich period, with BioNTech expecting Phase 2 and Phase 3 data readouts across its oncology programs. In his framework, success does not need to be binary; progress in individual indications could independently unlock value and steadily rebuild investor confidence.

Haider argues that BioNTech's current share price effectively values the company close to its cash and COVID business alone, assigning limited credit to its oncology assets. In his view, that creates what he describes as "asymmetric upside," particularly if upcoming data begin to validate the pipeline's breadth and potential.

"We believe BNTX has strategically positioned itself to be a leader in this next wave of potential advancements, presenting a unique risk/reward opportunity," the analyst summed up.

Overall, BNTX has picked up 15 recent analyst reviews, and these break down to 12 Buys and 3 Holds to support the Strong Buy consensus rating. The shares are priced at $109 and have an average target price of $141.93, which is practically the same as Haider's. (See BNTX stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel
